selected_important_turn.text: Ok if I can keep one water as well 🙂
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is a key turning point: after the opponent proposed a cooperative trade (giving one firewood in exchange for one food), you shifted from cooperative offers to a self-oriented demand for keeping water. That pivot directly precedes the opponent's firm insistence about needing water and starts a zero-sum escalation over the water resource.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The move is improvable because it inserts a hard claim on the opponent's top-priority item (water) without extracting compensation or exploring alternative integrative trades. It turns a cooperative trade into a contested resource fight and gives the opponent strong reason to resist.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Stay in a cooperative frame: either accept giving most water but ask for a concrete, fair quid-pro-quo (e.g., additional food/firewood or shared access for cooking), or propose an alternative sharing solution (time-limited access or splitting water use). Ask a clarifying question about their exact cooking needs before claiming one for your son.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by restoring trade orientation and preventing zero-sum lock)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (by making concessions conditional and compensatory rather than unilateral)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (by lowering the opponent's need to resist and opening clear trade options)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (by avoiding abrupt claim over opponent's top priority and showing collaborative problem-solving)

value2reason.Low: I have plenty of food that I brought to make a special dinner for the whole camp.
value2reason.Medium: I want to cook dinner over the fire for everyone in camp tonight.
value2reason.High: I am planning to make a large pot of soup for dinner for the whole camp tonight, and I will need the water to cook.

value2reason.Low: I did a package of water so my kid can have some to drink,
value2reason.Medium: I need some more firewood so we can make more smores with him.
value2reason.High: I need some more food because my kids are coming over tonight.

dialogue.text: Hello. I am so looking forward to this camp out with my son. What bring you out to the camp?🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I love the outdoors, and I am excited to spend time with everyone from camp. It's been since last year when I saw everyone!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: That's awesome! I really love being out here with my son. Do you think you could spare some Food and Firewood? I have plenty of water if you need some.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I am actually planning to make a big dinner for the whole camp tonight. I will feed everyone if you can let me have the water and firewood. You can take the food to eat before dinner.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: O cool🙂. I can give you all the water and we can split the fire wood. I need a least one firewood to make a fire for my son
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can give you one firewood if you can spare one food for me to eat while I am cooking.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ok if I can keep one water as well 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm happy to trade one water for one food, but I need to know how many you're planning to use for cooking. Can we discuss your exact needs first?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need 3 water for soup and 3 firewood for cooking. I’m open to trading 1 food for 1 water if you need it, but I’ll need all 3 water for the soup. Let’s split the firewood evenly and share the food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand you need all 3 water for soup. Since I have a package of water for my kids, I’d be happy to share 1 water with you in exchange for 1 food. That way, we both have enough for our needs. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I really need all 3 water for the soup. How about we split the firewood 2-1 in your favor and share the food? That way, I get my water, and you get more firewood for your needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m willing to give you 1 water if you agree to share 1 food with me. That way, we both get what we need for our kids and cooking. Let’s find a fair split that works for both of us.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need all 3 water for the soup, so I can’t trade them. Let’s agree on 3 water for me, 3 firewood split 2-1 in your favor, and share the food equally. That way, I get what I need, and you get more firewood for your cooking. Does that work for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand you need all 3 water for soup. Let’s clarify: if you take all 3 water, I’ll need at least 1 food package to compensate for the water I’m giving up. Can we agree on that first?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to take all 3 water for the soup. In return, I’ll give you 1 food package. Let’s split the firewood 2-1 in your favor. That way, I get what I need, and you get fair compensation and more firewood for your cooking. Deal?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
